Find Emission Peaks

Source: R/peaks.R
ls_find_peaks.Rd

Detects peaks in a LIBS spectrum using local maxima detection with prominence and signal-to-noise filtering.

Usage

ls_find_peaks(
  x,
  snr_threshold = 3,
  min_prominence = 0.01,
  min_distance_nm = 0.5
)

Arguments

x

A ls_spectrum() object.

snr_threshold

Numeric. Minimum signal-to-noise ratio. Default 3.

min_prominence

Numeric. Minimum peak prominence as a fraction of maximum intensity (0-1). Default 0.01.

min_distance_nm

Numeric. Minimum distance between peaks (nm). Default 0.5.

Value

A tibble::tibble() with columns wavelength_nm, intensity, snr, prominence, fwhm_nm, area, sorted by intensity descending.

Examples

spec <- ls_simulate_spectrum(elements = c(Ca = 5000, Na = 1000), seed = 1)
peaks <- ls_find_peaks(spec, snr_threshold = 5)
head(peaks)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 6
#>   wavelength_nm intensity   snr prominence fwhm_nm  area
#>           <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>
#> 1          240.      98.7  67.4       9.70      NA     0
#> 2          202.      97.1  65.1       2.03      NA     0
#> 3          206.      95.7  63.0       1.56      NA     0
#> 4          208.      95.7  63.0       1.04      NA     0
#> 5          205.      95.4  62.6       1.23      NA     0
#> 6          212.      94.9  61.8       1.59      NA     0
